column1: # Tier-0 in-silico grounding for LVAD scenario (3) AAV_BTR_GENE_THERAPY.tape (3) structurally-external limits.

column2: ~30% of injected dose in liver at day 1 (intravenous, mouse; majority extracellular not internalized)
column3: Wang/Li/Sadekar/Kamath/Shen (Genentech) 2024, Molecular Therapy: Methods & Clinical Development
column6: Radiolabeled (125I/111In dual-isotope) AAV9 in C57BL/6 mice. Liver is the dominant sink among organs; the tape's "~70%" figure is a common heuristic but the directly-measured %ID here is ~30% with most capsid extracellular. Species = mouse, not human. Anchors "liver is the dominant biodistribution sink" qualitatively; exact human %ID unmeasured.

column2: 44%-60% (10-country adult range, 1:1 serum dilution); pooled adult AAV9 = 57.8%
column3: Chhabra et al. 2024, Molecular Therapy: Methods & Clinical Development (global seroprevalence study, n=552; 502 adults)
column6: Cell-based transduction-inhibition NAb assay. Lowest = Australia ~44%, highest = South Korea ~60% at 1:1 dilution. Falls at the high end of the tape's "30-50%" heuristic; substantial geographic + dilution-cutoff dependence. Independent corroboration: Japanese healthy 36.5% (lower-stringency cohorts as low as 4.3% in German adult SMA, >1:50 cutoff).

column2: <1% of cardiomyocytes contained a vector genome in human heart (AAV1/SERCA2a, CUPID2b); AAV9 human quantitative efficiency unreported
column3: Greenberg et al. 2016 CUPID2b, Lancet (vg-detection reported via Bass-Zubek/Chamberlain review, PMC5544589)
column6: Best available human cardiac AAV transduction datum is AAV1 (not AAV9): CUPID2b post-hoc found <1% cardiomyocytes vg-positive, consistent with trial null result. AAV9 human cardiac efficiency is explicitly "very limited" in the literature; the tape's "<30%" is a conservative upper heuristic. Rodent AAV9 reaches ~96% (mouse) but drops sharply in large animals/humans. Confidence MED: serotype-mismatched proxy + qualitative human consensus.

column2: AB-1002 cardiotropic AAV2i8 / I-1c transgene, nonischemic NYHA-III HFrEF (LVEF 15-35%), Phase 1 (NCT04179643), n=11
column6: Cardiac-targeted AAV gene therapy trials DO exist and have reached Phase 1; NOT LMNA-related (I-1c PP1-inhibitor, contractility). No treatment-related AEs reported; preliminary LV-function improvement at 12mo. In-silico note: existence/phase only - NOT an efficacy or BTR claim (g8/f2).

column2: AskBio LION-CS101 (cited in tape S5 as "LMNA Phase I") is for LGMD2I/R9 (AB-1003), NOT LMNA dilated cardiomyopathy; NCT05230459
column3: AskBio press release / ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05230459 (via search corpus)
column6: Correction datum: no verified LMNA-DCM-specific AAV cardiac gene-therapy clinical trial was confirmed in this grounding pass. LION-CS101 = limb-girdle muscular dystrophy 2I/R9. Recorded here as honest disambiguation; S5 of the tape is NOT edited (S1-S8 untouched per task constraint) - flagged in ## Log only.
